response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has an extensive and impressive software career spanning 15+ years, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The single hardest blocker is the native-level Japanese language requirement, which is entirely absent from his profile. Beyond that, his technical background is overwhelmingly in web, TypeScript/Node.js, and full-stack development — not embedded C, Linux kernel internals, or network security. While he has peripheral mentions of C/C++ (NI LabWindows, bachelor thesis) and Linux usage, there is no evidence of kernel-level work, security protocol implementation (MACsec, 802.1X, etc.), or embedded networking experience. He does not meet the core required qualifications for this position.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience across diverse domains, demonstrating adaptability and breadth
Some exposure to low-latency, systems-level programming (DLang async I/O at Sociomantic; C/C++ at NI and in bachelor thesis)
Familiarity with Linux environments and some hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms, embedded mention at Altom)
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a hard, non-negotiable requirement for this role
No experience with network security technologies relevant to the role (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, switch chip SDKs, L2/L3 networking)
Primary expertise is web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB); embedded C and Linux kernel work are absent from his skill set

key_factors.factor: No embedded C / Linux kernel expertise
